title: USS Gerald Ford carrier strike group departs Middle East as US maintains two-carrier posture
summary: The USS Gerald R. Ford left the Middle East after extended operations against Iran, leaving USS Abraham Lincoln and USS George HW Bush among approximately 20 US warships maintaining the naval blockade and regional presence.
contents:
## USS Ford Departs Middle East After Extended Deployment

### Carrier Rotation
The USS Gerald R. Ford carrier strike group departed the Middle East on May 2 after participating in operations against Iran, according to US officials. The carrier originally left the United States in June 2025 and had its deployment extended twice during the conflict.

### Remaining Force Posture
Two aircraft carriers — USS Abraham Lincoln and USS George H.W. Bush — remain in the region among approximately 20 US warships. This configuration maintains the US naval blockade of Iranian ports and provides continued strike capability. Mark Cancian of the Center for Strategic and International Studies noted that US military capability "has not changed" as Washington returns to its typical two-carrier posture.

### Blockade Impact
The US military's blockade of Iranian ports has prevented an estimated $6 billion in oil exports since implementation. The Pentagon reports the blockade represents a key pressure point in negotiations, though Iran maintains its own closure of the Strait of Hormuz in response.

### Crew Fatigue Factor
Cancian explained the departure primarily reflects operational necessity: "The Ford carrier group had left the United States last June, and its deployment has extended twice. The crew and the ship need rest, so the United States sends the group home." Approximately 50,000 US service members remain deployed across the Middle East.

**Source:** Al Jazeera Iran War Day 64 summary
